items.answer_explanation: The move Nxc6 wins material by capturing the strong centralized White knight on c6, which puts pressure on White's pawn structure and opens lines for Black's active pieces. After Nxc6, if White recaptures with the b-pawn (bxc6), Black gains control over the c6 square and opens the b-file for the rook on b8. If White captures with the queen (Qxc6), White loses the queen, resulting in a decisive material advantage for Black. Additionally, this move removes a key defender of the e5 pawn and disrupts White's coordination.

items.answer_explanation: The move Bxf7+ is a strong tactical move because it forces the Black king to move, potentially exposing it to further attack. After Bxf7+, Black has two main responses: Kg8 or Kh7. If Black plays Kg8, White can follow up with Re7+ Kf8 Rcf7, putting pressure on the pinned e-pawn and gaining a clear material advantage. If Black plays Kh7, White can continue with Bd6+ Kh8 Bf4, attacking the pinned h6 pawn while maintaining pressure. In both cases, White gains a significant advantage by leveraging the power of the bishop and the rooks.
